title: [InstCombine] Re-queue users of phi when nsw/nuw flags of add are inferred
summary:
The generalization strategy identifies a recurring pattern where induction variable (IV) users need re-evaluation after inferring new `nuw`/`nsw` flags on the IV's step instruction. Initially observed in a specific test case with complex control flow, the developers abstracted the core insight: when an `add` instruction is part of a simple recurrence (`matchSimpleRecurrence`), and its flags are improved (`Changed`), users of the associated PHI node may now be optimizable.

The patch adds a lightweight check in `visitAdd` to detect this scenario and re-enqueue the PHI's users, enabling follow-on transforms (like trunc elimination) without adding compile-time overhead for non-IV adds. This transforms a one-off fix into a reusable rule that applies to any loop where IV step flags are strengthened, as demonstrated by the new generalized test cases.

**Key takeaway**: When specializing an optimization, look for the underlying IR pattern and a minimal, efficient hook point to trigger broader improvements.
